zotero.abstractNote: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in understanding visual content by aligning visual and textual representations. However, these models often rely on large-scale pre-training and complex architectures, making them computationally expensive and difficult to interpret. In this work, we propose DeViL, a novel approach to decode vision features into language. Instead of relying on end-to-end training, we leverage a pre-trained vision encoder and a frozen language model, and learn a lightweight mapping module to bridge the gap between visual and textual modalities. Our method achieves competitive performance on various vision-language tasks while being significantly more efficient and interpretable than existing approaches.
